source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Sir Lancelot rides slowly out of the castle at Camelot and onto the drawbridge that passes over the moat. Unbeknownst to him, his enemies have partially severed the vertical cable holding up the front end of the bridge so that it will break under a tension of \( 5.80 \times 10^3\, \text{N} \). The bridge has mass \( 200\, \text{kg} \) and its center of gravity is at its center. Lancelot, his lance, his armor, and his horse together have a combined mass of \( 600\, \text{kg} \). How far from the castle end of the bridge will the center of gravity of the horse plus rider be when the cable breaks?

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is registered as image-reliant
image_manifest contains 1 existing PNG asset, so a diagram is part of the problem package
question_text uses spatial/mechanical setup terms such as 'castle end', 'front end of the bridge', and a 'vertical cable', which are typically diagram-supported
multi_step: question_text describes a statics scenario requiring combination of multiple quantities: bridge mass, rider+horse mass, cable breaking tension, and positions
solving requires at least setting up torque/force balance and then isolating the unknown distance
question_char_length=601, consistent with a nontrivial physics word problem rather than a single-step lookup
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and answer_text='9.84m' provide a specific numeric target
choices are provided and include the stated answer, making final-answer checking straightforward
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true indicate the sample is structurally complete for evaluation

decision_reasons: asset registry passed with no recorded issues
question, answer, and 1 existing image asset are all present, so core semantics appear complete
initial scores are strong for image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability

the only noted risk is that the image was not directly inspected, but this alone is not enough to reject or downgrade a structurally complete sample

agent_assessment.rationale: The text clearly states the physics setup and asks for the rider-horse center-of-gravity position when the cable tension reaches its breaking value. The image is needed because it supplies the bridge geometry, especially the 12.0 m length and the placement of the cable and hinge, and it is semantically clear enough to support the problem.

cleaning_records.decision_rationale: The sample is jointly understandable, the required image support is clear enough, the rewritten open form preserves the same numeric target, and the alignment and solvability evidence show no serious failure for downstream annotation.
